title: passiflora rugosa var. rugosa
intro: Passiflora rugosa var. rugosa, also known as the wild passionflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids such as harman, harmine, and harmaline. The health benefits of Passiflora rugosa var. rugosa include reducing anxiety and insomnia, alleviating menopausal symptoms, and acting as an antispasmodic to ease muscle cramps. Herbal preparations of Passiflora rugosa var. rugosa can be found in teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
